claim: Across 50 integer Trans families with a2/b1^2 = -2/9 (b1 in {3,6,9,12,15}, a2 = -2 b1^2/9), the sum of indicial exponents (alpha_+ + alpha_-) takes 3 distinct values (-3, -1, +1) determined by s = a1/b1; the product takes >= 9 distinct values determined by r = b0/b1 and s; the discriminant takes >= 8 distinct values. None is a Trans invariant.

Of {|mu+/mu-|, |Im(alpha+)|, alpha_sum, alpha_product, alpha_disc, a2/b1^2}, only |mu+/mu-| (= (13+3 sqrt17)/4 ~ 6.34233) and a2/b1^2 (= -2/9) are constant across all 50 Trans families.  Log and Alg families have different |mu+/mu-| values (Log: {5.83, 6.85, 10.91, 14.43, 17.94}; Alg: 1).

After substituting a2 = -2 b1^2/9 and parameterising (b0, a1) = (r b1, s b1), the indicial sum becomes S(r,s) = (s + 2 b1/9)/(-(2/9)) (treating b1 dimensionally) = -(9 s + 2 b1)/2 ... up to dimensional rescaling: S, P, D have non-zero partial derivatives in r and s, hence are NON-CONSTANT on the Trans locus.

PSLQ at dps=150 on the basis [1, sqrt(17), |mu+/mu-|] returns the integer relation [13, 3, -4] with L-coefficient = -4 (non-zero), confirming  |mu+/mu-| = (13 + 3 sqrt(17))/4  to > 100 digits.  Wider PSLQ basis [1, sqrt2, sqrt3, sqrt5, sqrt17, pi, log2, ratio] returns [-13, 0, 0, 0, -3, 0, 0, 4] (same identity, L-coeff = 4, phantom-hit rule passes).

q := mu_+ / mu_- = -(13 + 3 sqrt(17))/4 (Trans signed branch) gives a2/b1^2 = q/(1+q)^2 = -2/9 exactly (sympy simplify).  The positive branch q = +(13 + 3 sqrt(17))/4 gives a2/b1^2 = +2/17, a different locus.  Therefore the iff holds iff one adds the sign condition a2 < 0.

The Birkhoff leading-order indicial framework provides NO new structural invariant on the Trans locus a2/b1^2 = -2/9 beyond the characteristic-root-ratio identity (which is algebraically equivalent to the Trans definition itself).  Indicial sum/product/discriminant vary across the 50 families.  The reason a2/b1^2 = -2/9 produces transcendental limits is therefore NOT captured by leading-order asymptotics.
